title: cnicus heterolepis
intro: Cnicus heterolepis, also known as knapweed or rosin root, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The roots of the plant are used for their health benefits, which include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and supporting the immune system. Cnicus heterolepis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
